dispatch_app package¶

Subpackages¶

  • dispatch_app.management package
    • Submodules
    • dispatch_app.management.permissions_setup module
      • create_application_groups()
    • Module contents
  • dispatch_app.migrations package
    • Submodules
    • dispatch_app.migrations.0001_initial module
      • Migration
        • Migration.dependencies
        • Migration.initial
        • Migration.operations
    • Module contents

Submodules¶

dispatch_app.admin module¶

Administrative configuration for the dispatch_app. Defines how models are displayed and managed within the Django Admin interface.

class dispatch_app.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Extends the default UserAdmin to include custom fields like ‘role’ and subscription relationships in the admin dashboard.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'usable_password', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('Role & Subscriptions', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role & Subscriptions', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))¶
list_display = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff']¶
property media¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

dispatch_app.api_views module¶

Application Programming Interface views for the dispatch_app. Handles the retrieval and filtering of articles for third-party clients based on user subscriptions.

class dispatch_app.api_views.ArticleViewSet(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ReadOnlyModelViewSet

API endpoint that allows articles to be viewed. Filters results based on the user’s subscriptions and optional search parameters.

basename = None¶
description = None¶
detail = None¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Filters the articles to show only those from journalists or publishers that the current user has subscribed to.

name = None¶
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]¶
serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

suffix = None¶

dispatch_app.apps module¶

Configuration module for the dispatch_app.

class dispatch_app.apps.DispatchAppConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]¶

Bases: AppConfig

Configuration class for the dispatch_app Django application.

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'¶
name = 'dispatch_app'¶
ready()[source]¶

Executed when the application starts. Connects signals and permission setups.

dispatch_app.models module¶

Models for the dispatch_app application. Defines the structure for Users, Publishers, Articles, and Newsletters.

class dispatch_app.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Represents a news article written by a journalist. Requires editor approval before becoming visible to readers.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id¶
content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_next_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶
publisher¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id¶
title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

updated_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class dispatch_app.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model supporting roles: Reader, Editor, and Journalist. Includes subscription fields for readers and publication tracking for journalists.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated¶

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

ROLE_CHOICES = (('READER', 'Reader'), ('EDITOR', 'Editor'), ('JOURNALIST', 'Journalist'))¶
articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

date_joined¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

email¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=True, **kwargs)¶
get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶
get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)¶
groups¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_active¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_staff¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_superuser¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

journalist_subscribers¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

last_name¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

In the example:

class dispatch_app.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Represents a news organization or publishing entity. Can have multiple affiliated editors and journalists.

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

dispatch_app.serializers module¶

Serializers for the dispatch_app application. Converts model instances into JavaScript Object Notation format and validates incoming data for the Representational State Transfer Application Programming Interface.

class dispatch_app.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializes article information, including nested author and publisher details for a comprehensive view.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Meta configuration for ArticleSerializer.

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'publisher', 'is_approved', 'created_at', 'updated_at']¶
model¶

alias of Article

class dispatch_app.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializes newsletter information for subscribers.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Meta configuration for NewsletterSerializer.

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'created_at']¶
model¶

alias of Newsletter

class dispatch_app.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializes publisher information.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Meta-configuration for PublisherSerializer.

fields = ['id', 'name']¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

class dispatch_app.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializes basic user information for public display.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Meta configuration for UserSerializer.

fields = ['id', 'username', 'first_name', 'last_name', 'role']¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

dispatch_app.signals module¶

Signals for the dispatch_app application. Handles automated tasks like email notifications and X integration when an article is approved.

dispatch_app.signals.handle_article_approval(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]¶

Triggered whenever an Article is saved. If approved, it initiates notifications and social sharing.

dispatch_app.signals.post_to_x(article)[source]¶

Uses the X Application Programming Interface to share the article. Requires OAuth1 authentication.

dispatch_app.signals.send_approval_emails(article)[source]¶

Collects recipients and sends an email notification about the newly published article.

dispatch_app.unit_tests module¶

Unit tests for the dispatch_app application. Specifically tests the RESTful API endpoints and ensures that article filtering based on subscriptions is functioning correctly.

class dispatch_app.unit_tests.ArticleAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]¶

Bases: APITestCase

Test suite for the Article Application Programming Interface.

setUp()[source]¶

Sets up the test environment by creating publishers, journalists, readers, and articles.

test_subscription_filtering()[source]¶

Tests that a reader only sees articles from sources they are subscribed to.

test_superuser_access()[source]¶

Ensures that superusers can see all approved articles regardless of subscriptions.

test_unauthenticated_access()[source]¶

Ensures that unauthenticated users cannot access the API.

dispatch_app.urls module¶

URL routing for the dispatch_app application.

dispatch_app.views module¶

Views for the dispatch_app application. Handles the logic for displaying articles and newsletters on the website front-end.

class dispatch_app.views.AboutView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: TemplateView

Displays information about the Dispatch Django platform.

template_name = 'dispatch_app/about.html'¶
class dispatch_app.views.ArticleCreateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, CreateView

Allows journalists to submit new articles for approval.

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']¶
form_valid(form)[source]¶

Automatically assigns the logged-in user as the author.

get_form(form_class=None)[source]¶

Customizes the form labels and choice options.

model¶

alias of Article

success_url = '/dashboard/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/article_form.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Ensures only journalists can create articles.

class dispatch_app.views.ArticleDeleteView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, DeleteView

Allows journalists to delete their own articles.

model¶

alias of Article

success_url = '/dashboard/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/article_confirm_delete.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Only the author (journalist) can delete their article.

class dispatch_app.views.ArticleDetailView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: DetailView

Displays the full content of a single article.

model¶

alias of Article

template_name = 'dispatch_app/article_detail.html'¶
class dispatch_app.views.ArticleListView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ListView

Displays a list of all approved articles with search functionality.

context_object_name = 'articles'¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Retrieves articles, filtering by approval status and optional search query.

model¶

alias of Article

template_name = 'dispatch_app/article_list.html'¶
class dispatch_app.views.ArticleUpdateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, UpdateView

Allows journalists to edit their own articles.

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']¶
model¶

alias of Article

success_url = '/dashboard/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/article_form.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Only the author (journalist) can edit their article.

class dispatch_app.views.CustomUserCreationForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: UserCreationForm

Custom form for user registration that includes the functional role selection.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Meta-configuration for the registration form.

fields = ('username', 'role')¶
model¶

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.TypedChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class dispatch_app.views.EditorDashboardView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, ListView

Displays all articles that are pending approval for Editors to review.

context_object_name = 'pending_articles'¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Retrieves articles that have not yet been approved.

model¶

alias of Article

template_name = 'dispatch_app/editor_dashboard.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Ensures only editors can access the approval dashboard.

class dispatch_app.views.HomeView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: TemplateView

Displays the landing page of the application with options to register or log in.

template_name = 'dispatch_app/home.html'¶
class dispatch_app.views.JournalistDashboardView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, ListView

Displays a list of articles authored by the logged-in journalist.

context_object_name = 'user_articles'¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Retrieves only articles authored by the current user.

model¶

alias of Article

template_name = 'dispatch_app/journalist_dashboard.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Ensures only journalists can access this view.

class dispatch_app.views.NewsletterCreateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, CreateView

Allows journalists to write and publish newsletters.

fields = ['title', 'content']¶
form_valid(form)[source]¶

Assigns the journalist as the author automatically.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

success_url = '/newsletters/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/newsletter_form.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Ensures only journalists can create newsletters.

class dispatch_app.views.NewsletterDeleteView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, DeleteView

Allows journalists to delete their own newsletters.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

success_url = '/newsletters/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/newsletter_confirm_delete.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Only the author (journalist) can delete their newsletter.

class dispatch_app.views.NewsletterListView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, ListView

Displays newsletters for readers or the journalist’s own newsletters.

context_object_name = 'newsletters'¶
get_queryset()[source]¶

Filters newsletters based on the current user role.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

template_name = 'dispatch_app/newsletter_list.html'¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, UpdateView

Allows journalists to edit their own newsletters.

model¶

success_url = '/newsletters/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/newsletter_form.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Only the author (journalist) can edit their newsletter.

class dispatch_app.views.PublisherCreateView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, CreateView

Allows editors to create a new publisher entry.

fields = ['name']¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

success_url = '/publishers/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/publisher_form.html'¶
test_func()[source]¶

Ensures only editors can create publishers.

class dispatch_app.views.PublisherListView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, ListView

Lists all publishers so users can join one.

context_object_name = 'publishers'¶
model¶

alias of Publisher

template_name = 'dispatch_app/publisher_list.html'¶
class dispatch_app.views.RegisterView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: CreateView

Handles user registration. Uses a custom form if needed, but for now, we extend the basic UserCreationForm.

form_class¶

alias of CustomUserCreationForm

model¶

alias of CustomUser

success_url = '/login/'¶
template_name = 'dispatch_app/register.html'¶
class dispatch_app.views.SubscriptionsView(**kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, TemplateView

Allows readers to view and manage their subscriptions to journalists and publishers.

get_context_data(**kwargs)[source]¶

Adds available journalists and publishers to the template context.

post(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Handles the subscription toggle logic.

template_name = 'dispatch_app/subscriptions.html'¶
dispatch_app.views.approve_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Function-based view that allows an editor to approve an article. Flips the ‘is_approved’ flag to True.

dispatch_app.views.join_publisher(request, pk)[source]¶

Adds the current editor or journalist to the chosen publisher.

dispatch_app.views.reject_article(request, pk)[source]¶

Allows an editor to unpublish an article, moving it back to the review queue.

Module contents¶

Dispatch Django
